A R G U E D; THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: CONTINUED TO JANUARY 5, 2012. SUPPLEMENTAL PAPERS TO BE FILED BY MR. LEVY ON THE STATUTORY AUTHORITY OF DEPOSITING THE $100,000.00 IN COURT THROUGH AN INTERPLEADER ACTION BY DECEMBER 16, 2011. MR. HARRIS AND THE VA TO FILE THEIR OPPOSITION AND TO INDICATE HOW TO RESOLVE THE DISPUTE BY DECEMBER 28, 2011. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALBERT LEVY MOTION FOR ORDER TO DISCHARGE DEFENDANT STAKEHOLDER. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. THE RELIEF REQUESTED BY THE MOTION GIVES EFFECT TO THE ORDER ENFORCING THE SETTLEMENT FILED JUNE 2, 2011, WHILE PROVIDING A MEANS AND INDUCEMENT FOR THE PLAINTIFF AND THE VA TO ADJUDICATE THE VALIDITY OF THE VA LIEN. PLAINTIFF AND THE VA SHOULD MEET AND CONFER TO DETERMINE IF THEY CAN AGREE THAT ANY OR ALL OF THE SETTLEMENT MONIES TO BE PAID BY DEFENDANT IN EXCESS OF THE VA'S LIEN CAN BE PAID DIRECTLY TO PLAINTIFF RATHER THAN DEPOSITED IN COURT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

Off Calendar Jan-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ALBERT LEVY MOTION FOR ORDER TO DISCHARGE DEFENDANT STAKEHOLDER.
Appearances: Eric Gale of Burke Williams & Sorensen LLP (650.327.2672) for defendant and moving party; Michael Harris (no card provided) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona; Darrell M. McDonald (750.2288) from the Department of Veterans Affairs.
Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. The relief requested by the motion gives effect to the order enforcing the settlement filed June 2, 2011, while providing a means and inducement for the plaintiff and the VA to adjudicate the validity of the VA lien. Plaintiff and the VA should meet and confer to determine if they can agree that any or all of the settlement monies to be paid by defendant in excess of the VA's lien can be paid directly to plaintiff rather than deposited in court. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
